So apparently when Trump mentions tariffs, global markets immediately have a collective panic attack and everyone starts hoarding gold like it's the apocalypse. Because nothing says 'rational economic response' like fleeing to precious metals the second someone tweets about trade policy.

Major automakers just straight up stopped making cars because their supply chains cross more borders than a spy novel. Turns out when you build cars using parts from seventeen different countries, trade wars make things a teensy bit complicated.

Grocery stores are now playing musical suppliers as food import costs shoot up faster than a SpaceX rocket. Your weekly shopping bill is about to look like a mortgage payment, but hey, at least the economy is 'winning.'

Restaurant owners are discovering that when ingredient costs go through the roof, profit margins go through the floor. It's almost like the food service industry operates on razor-thin budgets or something, who could have predicted this shocking development.

Local taco shops are now charging enough for a burrito to fund a small space mission. Remember when tacos were affordable comfort food and not luxury dining experiences that required taking out a second mortgage.
Food delivery apps are watching their order volume drop faster than my motivation on Monday morning. Apparently people draw the line at paying thirty dollars for mediocre pad thai that arrives cold.

And here we are, the final domino, Karen's overpriced burrito showing up late and lukewarm, and I am ABSOLUTELY LOSING MY MIND over the fact that they put the cilantro directly on the rice instead of keeping it separate like any civilized human being would do, because apparently we live in a world where basic burrito construction principles mean NOTHING!
